claim: Three vowel-pattern rules survive honest held-out validation at 70%+ precision: '_.o._.o' -> herbal (82.4% precision on 34 held-out fires), '_._.eee' -> recipes (72.7% on 11 fires), '_.e.ai' -> recipes (70.6% on 17 fires). These three rules together cover 0.8% of held-out tokens at 77.4% aggregate precision. They are Brain-V's first honestly-validated decipherment fragments.
evidence_for: 80/20 folio-stratified split with 181 train / 45 holdout folios
3 rules pass held-out >=0.70 precision AND >=10 fires
Sparse dictionary covers 62/8008 held-out tokens at 77.4% precision
All three rules have train-set precision comparable to or below held-out precision (no overfitting)
evidence_against: Earlier headline rule '_.eo -> pharmaceutical' did NOT survive this stricter test
11-fire and 17-fire supports are modest; a 4th fold might remove borderline rules
test: Re-split with different random seed and retest; the 3 rules should survive
test_threshold: At least 2 of the 3 rules survive a second 80/20 split at >=0.65 precision

tests_run.details: 3/103 candidate rules survive >=0.70 precision + >=10 fires on stratified holdout. Aggregate precision 77.4% on 0.8% coverage.

tests_remaining: Look for semantic interpretation (what does _.o._.o 'mean' if herbal?)
Expand beyond section metadata to image-label targets
reasoning: Narrow, honest, replicable. The sparse high-precision path was the correct successor after H-BV-VOWEL-CODE-01 failed its held-out test. Three rules is three more than any coverage-game methodology has produced.
